findings.summary: Description engagement question does not match the on-screen #cta-question verbatim
findings.evidence: Spoken closer (script.txt line 15): "Are you fixing that today with one config line? Or accepting the waste?" On-screen #cta-question (scene-cta.html line 95): "One config line — or accept the waste?" Description (youtube-description.md line 34): "Are you fixing that today with one config line, or accepting the waste?" The description and spoken versions differ from the on-screen version in phrasing. The on-screen version is significantly shorter/different. All three surfaces should reference the exact same question for maximum alignment.
findings.fix: Align all three surfaces. The on-screen #cta-question has the most compressed, punchiest form: "One config line — or accept the waste?" Update the description's engagement paragraph to: "One config line — or accept the waste? Drop your pick in the comments." Alternatively, decide on the spoken version as canonical and update #cta-question to match. Either is valid — but all three must agree.

findings.summary: Body bullets use → arrow characters (7 instances); YouTube may flag or hide descriptions containing them
findings.evidence: Lines 20-26 each begin with '→'. Line 24 additionally contains two inline '→' characters: 'parse → match patterns on nodes → Rust multi-core'. Total: 9 arrow characters across 7 lines.
findings.fix: Replace leading '→' with a plain '-' or remove entirely (YouTube renders the bullet-list paragraph as plain text anyway). For inline arrows on line 24, rewrite to: 'parse, match patterns on nodes, then Rust multi-core'. Confirm the rule at §13 of youtube-metadata.md.

findings.summary: vidIQ keyword research artifact is missing — no research/vidiq-keywords.md found
findings.evidence: Glob over videos/ast-grep-missing-layer-short/research/ returned no files. The mandatory pre-description vidIQ keyword research pass (per youtube-metadata.md 'MANDATORY: vidIQ Keyword Research Before Writing') was not run or not saved. Cannot verify that hashtags and keywords were derived from real search-demand data.
findings.fix: Run the vidIQ MCP research pass (vidiq_keyword_research, vidiq_outliers, vidiq_trending_videos) for seed terms: 'ast-grep', 'AI coding agent', 'Claude Code', 'structural search', 'tree-sitter'. Save snapshot to videos/ast-grep-missing-layer-short/research/vidiq-keywords.md. Validate that the 23 current hashtags and first-200-char keywords align with the top shortlist terms. Adjust if needed before upload.
